$schema_note: v0 gold labels for MTA Example 1.pdf. Each entry's `clause_match` is a substring used to align the predicted clause to the gold (substring of the clause text, case-insensitive, first occurrence wins). Levels: green | amber | red | blue. `topic_id` references uoa_positions.json; null when topic is not covered. `notes` capture the human reasoning so the labels are auditable.
labelled_by: Claude (Opus 4.7) — v0 proposal, awaiting human review

labels.clause_match: will not use the Material for administration to human
labels.notes: Material use restriction (no human/clinical/diagnostic). UoA Positions does not cover material-use restrictions.

labels.clause_match: may use the Material and Data solely for the purposes of the Study
labels.notes: Approved-purpose / scope-of-use clause. Standard MTA term, not in UoA Positions.

labels.clause_match: use a courier with suitable skill and experience
labels.notes: Logistics — transport, risk passing, no charge. Not a UoA Positions topic.

labels.clause_match: no warranties are given about quality or fitness for a particular purpose
labels.notes: Provider's no-warranty disclaimer. Touches Warranties; symmetric to UoA's preferred 'as is' position. Acceptable but worth confirming UoA bears no implied warranty obligation.

labels.clause_match: ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk
labels.notes: Data security obligations on Recipient. Touches Confidentiality. Duration not stated — manager review.

labels.clause_match: shall keep all of the Data secure from any unauthorised
labels.notes: Access controls (employees/students under supervision). Not directly a UoA Positions topic.

labels.clause_match:
the Recipient
Institution assumes all liability for damages
labels.notes: 'Assumes all liability' is a deviation from POS-07 preferred (negligence-only), but cap of NZD$100,000 is well below the $500,000 preferred ceiling, so practical exposure is bounded. Borderline — escalate per Research Contract Delegations.

UoA indemnifies Provider 'in full' for ALL liability — broader than POS-08 acceptable scope (negligence/wilful misconduct of UoA only, with proportionate-reduction and mitigation carve-outs). Clear escalation.

labels.clause_match: will not extend to loss of business or profit
labels.notes: Mutual exclusion of indirect/consequential loss + loss of business/profit — exactly what POS-07 preferred requires.

labels.clause_match:
obtain the written consent of the Provider
Institution if there is any material change
labels.notes: Change-of-use consent. Operational MTA term, not a UoA Positions topic.

labels.clause_match: aim to co-author any publications arising from this Agreement
labels.notes: Co-authorship + acknowledgment + copy-of-publication only. No approval/veto/embargo, no stand-down — preserves PI's right to publish. Matches POS-12 preferred.

labels.clause_match: Provider Institution undertakes that where required by Applicable Laws
labels.notes: Provider retains ownership/custodian rights over Materials and Data. IP ownership clause — UoA Positions does not cover IP; refer to Auckland UniServices.

labels.clause_match:
shall keep all information relating to the Material and Data
confidential
labels.notes: Confidentiality with standard exclusions (public domain, independently developed, etc.) — exclusions match POS-11 preferred. But duration not specified in this clause; could be perpetual unless capped elsewhere → manager review.

labels.clause_match: results generated in the course of the Study
labels.notes: Joint ownership of foreground results + cross-consent for use. IP clause — refer to Auckland UniServices.

labels.clause_match: not to attempt to identify or contact any individual
labels.notes: Re-identification prohibition + breach-notification. Privacy/Data-handling, not in UoA Positions.

labels.clause_match:
Provider Institution from
being able to distribute the Material
labels.notes: Provider retains right to distribute Materials elsewhere. Not in UoA Positions.

labels.clause_match:
right to terminate this agreement forthwith at any
time
labels.notes: One-way termination by Provider for breach or ethics withdrawal. POS-19 preferred is mutual two-way termination — deviation. No payment-for-work-done addressed → escalation worthy.
